_description: Multi-layout data visualization template for 16:9 presentations. Each slide uses ONE layout type, activated by populating the corresponding slots. Supports: text (body paragraphs + bullets + callout), bar-chart (vertical bars), horizontal-bar, table, flow (process diagram), stat-grid (KPI cards), comparison (two-column pros/cons), timeline, and funnel. Every layout supports an optional image panel on the right side. Keep bg and ink consistent across all slides; vary only accent.

_slots.heading: REQUIRED — slide heading. 2–8 words. Displayed at ~56px.
_slots.subheading: optional — context line below heading. 1 sentence.
_slots.label: optional — uppercase eyebrow tag above heading (e.g. 'Q4 RESULTS', 'OVERVIEW').
_slots.footnote: optional — small footnote at bottom left (e.g. 'Source: internal data').
_slots.image: optional — image for any layout. Renders as a right-side panel (520px wide, rounded corners). When present, the data zone shrinks to make room. Works with all layouts. Accepts local path or URL.
_slots.image-caption: optional — small caption text below the image.
_slots.body: optional — body paragraph for text layout. 1–5 sentences. Presence of body WITHOUT any data-layout slots (bar, hbar, table, flow, stat, compare, timeline, funnel) activates the text layout.
_slots.body-2: optional — second paragraph for text layout.
_slots.bullet-1: optional — first bullet point. Short, 1 line. Up to 6 bullets.
_slots.callout: optional — highlighted callout box with left accent border. Italic. Good for key takeaways.
_slots.bar-label-1: optional — X-axis label for bar 1 (e.g. 'Q1'). Populating bar-label-1 activates the vertical bar chart. Up to 8 bars.
_slots.bar-value-1: optional — bar 1 height as number 0–100 (percentage of chart height).
_slots.bar-display-1: optional — display text above bar 1 (e.g. '$1.2M'). If empty, numeric value shown.
_slots.bar-label-2: optional — same pattern for bars 2–8. Only populate as many as you need.
_slots.hbar-label-1: optional — label for horizontal bar 1 (e.g. 'React'). Populating hbar-label-1 activates horizontal bar chart. Up to 6 bars.
_slots.hbar-display-1: optional — display text at end of bar (e.g. '85%'). If empty, value shown with '%'.
_slots.table-h-1: optional — first column header. Populating table-h-1 activates table layout. Up to 5 columns × 5 rows.
_slots.table-r1-c1: optional — row 1, column 1. First column is bold (row label). Other columns right-aligned monospace.
_slots.flow-step-1: optional — first step label. Populating flow-step-1 activates flow diagram. Up to 5 steps with arrows between them.
_slots.flow-desc-1: optional — short description under step 1. 1 sentence.
_slots.stat-value-1: optional — large stat number (e.g. '98%', '$4.2M'). Populating stat-value-1 activates stat grid. 2–4 stat cards.
_slots.compare-left-title: optional — left column title (e.g. 'Before'). Populating activates comparison layout. Up to 4 items per side.
_slots.compare-right-title: optional — right column title (e.g. 'After'). Right column is highlighted with accent border.
_slots.compare-left-1: optional — first item in left column (shown with ✕ bullet).
_slots.compare-right-1: optional — first item in right column (shown with ✓ bullet).
_slots.timeline-date-1: optional — date label for point 1 (e.g. '2020'). Populating activates timeline. Up to 5 points.
_slots.timeline-event-1: optional — event description for point 1. 1–2 sentences.
_slots.funnel-stage-1: optional — widest funnel stage label (e.g. 'Awareness'). Populating activates funnel. Up to 5 stages, narrowing progressively.
_slots.bg: optional — background hex color. Use the SAME value on every slide. Default: #0f0e0c
_slots.ink: optional — primary text hex color. Use the SAME value on every slide. Default: #f0ece4
_slots.accent: optional — accent hex color for bars, highlights, active elements. VARY per slide. Default: #e8b86d
_slots.accent-secondary: optional — second accent for comparison right column. If empty, accent is used.

title: Product Analytics — Q4 Review

slides.label: OVERVIEW
slides.heading: Why We're Rethinking Our Pipeline
slides.subheading: Three core problems surfaced in the Q3 retrospective
slides.body: Our current deployment pipeline was designed for a team of five. With 40 engineers shipping daily, the bottlenecks have become critical. Cycle time has tripled since January, and developer satisfaction scores are at an all-time low.
slides.body-2: This deck outlines the data behind the problem, the proposed solution, and the expected impact across all product teams.
slides.bullet-1: Average deploy time increased from 12 min to 47 min
slides.callout: If we don't act now, we'll lose our ability to ship fast — the one advantage that got us here.

slides.label: CONTEXT
slides.heading: The Team Behind the Numbers
slides.body: Our platform engineering team has grown from 5 to 18 engineers over the past year. The rapid scaling has introduced coordination challenges that the existing tooling wasn't designed to handle.

slides.label: REVENUE
slides.heading: Quarterly Revenue Growth
slides.subheading: Strong upward trend across all four quarters

slides.label: INVENTORY
slides.heading: Product Catalog Summary
slides.subheading: Top SKUs by category with current stock levels

slides.label: HISTORY
slides.heading: Company Milestones
slides.timeline-event-1: Founded in a garage with 3 engineers and a bold thesis
slides.timeline-event-2: Launched v1.0 — 500 beta users in the first month
slides.timeline-event-3: Series A: $12M raised, team grew to 25 people
slides.timeline-event-4: Crossed 10K paying customers, launched enterprise tier
slides.timeline-event-5: Series B: $40M raised, expanding to APAC and EMEA

slides.label: CONVERSION
slides.heading: Marketing Funnel Analysis
slides.subheading: From first touch to retained customer
